--- SOURCE 3: Biofertilizers aim to make global food production both sustainable ... ---
URL: https://www.dtu.dk/english/newsarchive/2025/07/biofertilizers-aim-to-make-global-food-production-both-sustainable-and-affordable

SUMMARY:
A new international research initiative aims to lessen our dependence on artificial fertilizers while promoting biotechnology industries in the Global South.

FULL CONTENT:
Biotechnology

# Biofertilizers aim to make global food production both sustainable and affordable

A new international research initiative aims to lessen our dependence on artificial fertilizers while promoting biotechnology industries in the Global South. Initiative for Biofertilizer Innovation and Science (IBIS), is funded by a total of DKK 215 million from the Novo Nordisk Foundation and the Gates Foundation.

![](/english/-/media/dtudk/nyheder/webnyheder/2025/07/ibis-web2.jpg?rw=960&rh=0&hash=C93A084CCC3431576DCEA628FA65896F)

**Wednesday 02 July 2025**

Simon Koefoed Toft

The growing world population needs a growing food production. And the food of the future must be produced more sustainably and with less environmental impact than is the case today.

IBIS, which will be headquartered at Technical University of Denmark (DTU), aims to enable solutions to these challenges using biofertilisers.

Biofertilizers are microorganisms such as fungi and bacteria that help plants absorb nutrients from the soil. And biofertilizers have a long list of advantages over the artificial fertilizers used today, says Rasmus J.N. Frandsen, Associate Professor at DTU Bioengineering and Director of IBIS:

"Some microorganisms can increase plant availability of soil-bound nutrients, while others convert atmospheric nitrogen into plant available ammonium. Collectively this can lower the requirement for artificial fertilizers and reduce harm to the environment. At the same time, biofertilizers can be produced locally and with much lower energy consumption than artificial fertilizers, making them both cheaper and more sustainable."

## We lack knowledge and free data

The biggest obstacle to the use of microbial biofertilizers on an industrial scale globally is the lack of data and understanding of how microorganisms behave in different field conditions. We don’t yet fully understand when and how biofertilizers work, how to document their properties, or how different manufacturing methods influence their performance.

To unlock the full potential of biofertilisers, the partner universities in the project, which come from Denmark, India and South Africa, will collaborate to conduct large-scale, standardized experiments to generate the very large data sets and deeper understanding that are needed to start actual production.

"Each partner has unique knowledge and resources that, when combined, enable us to write a ‘cookbook’ on how to develop biofertilizers. The purpose of the initiative is not to sell biofertilizer, but to provide the recipes and data sets that enable others to set up local production facilities around the world. And the cookbook will be free, in the sense that all the data we create will be freely available," explains Rasmus J.N. Frandsen about the partners and their mission.

## Benefits for small and large farms

With strong research groups in both the Global South and North, IBIS has a unique opportunity to contribute knowledge about the biology, production and performance of biofertilizers in the field that is lacking today.

In this way, IBIS underpins the Novo Nordisk Foundation's work to support society in the green transition, says Claus Felby, Vice President for Agri-Food at the Novo Nordisk Foundation:

"IBIS represents an important step towards promoting high-yield sustainable agriculture by creating the foundation for reliable biofertilizer solutions that benefit both small farmers and large agricultural operations worldwide.”

Fakta

IBIS is supported with a total of DKK 215 million over five years by the Novo Nordisk Foundation and the Gates Foundation.

The partners in the initiative are:

* Technical University of Denmark (DTU) (Denmark)
* University of Copenhagen (Denmark)
* Aarhus University (Denmark)
* Tamil Nadu Agricultural University (India)
* Council for Scientific and Industrial Research (South Africa)

DTU is a leader in biotechnology research and was named the third best university in this field by the Shanghai Rankings in 2022.

IBIS is headquartered at DTU Bioengineering and is led by Associate Professor Rasmus J. N. Frandsen.

Top 10 Biofertilizer Industry Solutions (2025 - 2033): That Trends and Solutions Impacting The Agriculture Industry
===================================================================================================================

RELEASE DATE:  Sep 2024 Author: Spherical Insights

_Explore Global biofertilizer trends and future prospects, offering data-driven insights and emerging technologies to guide critical business decisions._

* * *

[**Request Free Sample**](https://www.sphericalinsights.com/request-sample-blog/163)[**Speak to Analyst**](https://www.sphericalinsights.com/speak-to-analyst-blog/163)

Biofertilizers, also called bioinoculants, are living microorganisms that enhance plant nutrition by inhabiting the rhizosphere when introduced through soil or seeds. Organisms like cyanobacteria, soil bacteria that fix nitrogen, bacteria that solubilize phosphate, and molds are primarily used in making biological fertilizers.

**Global Biofertilizers Market Size Insights Forecasts to 2030**

*   The [Biofertilizers Market](https://www.sphericalinsights.com/reports/biofertilizers-market) Size was valued at USD 1.88 Billion in 2021.
*   The Market is growing at a CAGR of 11.87 % from 2022 to 2030
*   The Worldwide Biofertilizers Market Size is expected to reach USD 4.63 Billion by 2030
*   Asia Pacific is expected to grow the fastest during the forecast period

The report provides a summary of the global top trends and future prospects for the global biofertilizers market based on historical data sets (current trends, industry statistics, and emerging trends shaping the biofertilizers industry’s future, preliminary estimates from 2020-2021, 2022, and 2023), as well as projections for the global biofertilizers trends and future prospects for global regions in 2024 and 2025. This data-driven research offers users an industry-wide view of emerging technologies and innovative insights to help them make critical business decisions.

**Current Trends in the Biofertilizers Market**

**Growing Demand for Organic Farming**: The rising awareness of the benefits of organic farming and the demand for organic produce are driving the growth of the biofertilizers market. Consumers and farmers alike are increasingly prioritizing sustainability, which aligns with the natural benefits of biofertilizers.

**Technological Advancements**: Innovations in biotechnology and microbial science are enhancing the effectiveness of biofertilizers. Advanced formulations and delivery systems, such as encapsulation techniques and liquid formulations, are improving the stability and efficiency of these products.

**Government Initiatives and Policies:** Many governments worldwide are supporting sustainable agriculture through subsidies, incentives, and research funding for biofertilizer development. This regulatory support is creating a favorable environment for market growth.

**Increasing Awareness and Education**: Educational initiatives and research are raising awareness about the advantages of biofertilizers. Farmers are becoming more informed about the benefits of integrating biofertilizers into their farming practices.

**Opportunities in the Biofertilizers Market**

**Expanding Market Reach**: Emerging economies in Asia-Pacific, Latin America, and Africa are showing increasing adoption of biofertilizers. These regions present significant growth opportunities due to their vast agricultural sectors and the rising need for sustainable farming solutions.

**Product Innovation**: There is a growing demand for biofertilizers tailored to specific crops and soil conditions. Companies that invest in research and development to create customized solutions are likely to capture a larger market share.

**Integration with Precision Agriculture**: The integration of biofertilizers with precision agriculture technologies, such as soil sensors and GPS, can enhance their effectiveness and appeal to modern farmers seeking data-driven solutions.

**Collaborations and Partnerships**: Strategic partnerships between biofertilizer producers, agricultural research institutions, and government bodies can drive innovation and market expansion. Collaborations can also help in addressing challenges related to distribution and scalability.

**Top 5 Companies**

**1. Lallemand**

**![Image 3: Lallemand](https://www.sphericalinsights.com/images/blog/lallemand.png)**

Lallemand stands out worldwide for its work in creating and manufacturing cutting-edge microbial solutions, particularly in the field of fertilizers. The company is skilled in developing new products that improve both plant health and productivity. The company creates biocontrols and biofertilizers through its Lallemand Plant Care branch, using living microorganisms like bacteria and filamentous fungi to enhance soil fertility and increase crop production. By utilizing the latest research and top-of-the-line facilities, Lallemand is committed to promoting sustainable agricultural practices through offering eco-friendly solutions for contemporary farming obstacles.

**Lallemand Increases Research and Development Sites in France**

Lallemand introduced spacious new laboratories measuring 800 square meters in Blagnac, France on September 4, 2024, to enhance its bioprocess innovation and fertilizer development efforts. The investment of €1.7 million improves research for plant care and microbial solutions, bolstering worldwide R&D endeavours.

**2. IPL Biologicals**

![Image 4: IPL Biologicals](https://www.sphericalinsights.com/images/blog/ipl-biologicals.png)

IPL Biologicals is a key player in the agricultural biotechnology sector, focusing on creating and manufacturing bio-fertilizers and biopesticides. The company's main focus is utilizing advanced microbial technology to develop eco-friendly solutions that improve soil health and increase crop productivity. IPL Biologicals is committed to promoting sustainable agriculture by offering innovative products that enhance nutrient absorption and defend plants against pests and diseases. By focusing on research and development, IPL Biologicals strives to offer high-quality, efficient substitutes for traditional chemical inputs to support the worldwide transition to more sustainable farming methods.

**IPL Biologicals and Uzkimyosanoat have created a joint venture to introduce bio-fertilizers in Uzbekistan.**

IPL Biologicals has partnered with Uzkimyosanoat to launch and deliver bio-fertilizers in Uzbekistan through a joint venture. This collaboration seeks to improve the agricultural industry of the nation by offering innovative, environmentally friendly fertilizer options that enhance soil vitality and boost crop output. The partnership will center on utilizing IPL Biologicals' knowledge in bio-fertilizers and Uzkimyosanoat's understanding of the local market to address the increasing need for sustainable agricultural products in Uzbekistan.

**3. National Fertilizers Limited**

![Image 5: National Fertilizers Limited](https://www.sphericalinsights.com/images/blog/national-fertilizers-limited.png)

National Fertilizers Limited (India) is a prominent Indian state-owned company that specializes in the manufacturing and dispersal of fertilizers. Founded in 1974, the company's headquarters are located in Noida, Uttar Pradesh. NFL runs multiple manufacturing facilities in India, where they manufacture various chemical fertilizers like urea, DAP, and NPK fertilizers. NFL plays an important role in India's farming industry by providing essential nutrients that improve crop yields and soil fertility. The company is dedicated to providing affordable high-quality fertilizers to assist farmers in the country and encourage sustainable agricultural methods. Furthermore, the NFL is engaged in different efforts to enhance agricultural efficiency and support the country's food security.

**4. Bioceres S.A.**

![Image 6: Bioceres S.A.](https://www.sphericalinsights.com/images/blog/bioceres-sa.jpg)

Bioceres S.A. is an Argentine company that specializes in agricultural biotechnology. Established in 2001, the company focuses on creating and selling new technologies and products to improve crop productivity and sustainability. The company is recognized for its efforts in genetic engineering, biopesticides, and biofertilizers. One significant achievement is the creation of genetically modified seeds that have enhanced resistance to pests and environmental stress, resulting in higher agricultural yields. Bioceres also puts money into research and development to tackle issues in farming and encourage sustainable methods.

**5. BioWorks Inc**

![Image 7: 5.	BioWorks Inc ](https://www.sphericalinsights.com/images/blog/bioworks-inc.jpg)

BioWorks Inc. specializes in offering creative answers within the realm of biotechnology. Their focus is on creating and promoting products and technologies that tackle diverse issues in agriculture, horticulture, and environmental sustainability. Their range of products frequently consists of biopesticides, biofertilizers, and other biological products created to improve crop yield and safeguard against pests and diseases in an environmentally sustainable way.

**Top 6 Recent Developments**

*   **In May 2024,** Fresh Del Monte entered into a collaboration to manufacture biofertilizers from leftover fruit. The strategically positioned new biofertilizer plant is located near Del Monte Kenya Ltd, a subsidiary of Fresh Del Monte in Kenya. The company's pineapple cannery residues will be used by the plant to produce different biofertilizers for both internal use and for potential sale to other farmers in Kenya and East African nations.

*   **In January 2024,** the Indian Biogas Association (IBA), a non-profit organization located in Haryana, put forth a request for incentives for the "Biogas-Fertilizer Fund" in their wishlist for the Union Budget, 2024. The association's goal is to encourage the utilization of organically produced fertilizers and speed up the expansion of the bio compressed natural gas sector.

*   **In December 2023,** IPL Biologicals, a branch located in Gurugram, Haryana, entered into an agreement with the Gujarat Government to establish a new bio-pesticide and bio-fertilizer production plant in the state. The memorandum of understanding was signed for a sum of 4000 million and would concentrate on reducing the use of chemicals in the agriculture sector.

*   **In August 2023,** Bionema Group Ltd., a top biocontrol technology producer in the U.K., introduced four new biofertilizers for forestry, agriculture, and sport turf. The recently released BioNFix and Rhizosafe products contain Azospirillum lipoferum BNL714, Paenibacillus azotofixans BNL1913, and additional strains. They can help revive the soil's nutrient cycle, leading to enhanced plant growth.

*   **In January 2023,** Odisha University of Agriculture and Technology (OUAT) announced the development of liquid nitrogen bio fertilizers, specifically designed for pulses. The biofertilizer plant for production was also funded by almost USD 15 million from the Rashtriya Krishi Vikas Yojana (RKVY) at the university.

*   **In October 2022,** Brazilian researchers presented their development of biofertilizers made with nitrogen-fixing microorganisms. The main goal of this biofertilizer is to substitute the use of synthetic fertilizers in soybean farming.

enriched_points.expanded_content: Recent advancements in biotechnology focus on developing biofertilizers from microalgae and other microorganisms, enhancing soil fertility and promoting sustainable agricultural practices (Source 1).

Innovative research on microbial strains emphasizes their role in nutrient recycling, improving crop systems, and enhancing soil health, driving the adoption of biofertilizers in agriculture (Source 2).
Combining biofertilizers with precision agriculture technologies, such as soil sensors and data analytics, optimizes nutrient application and promotes efficient farming solutions (Source 4).
